channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former Obama adviser reflects on the 'Battle for American Identity'
item.description: Ben Rhodes was a speechwriter and security adviser for President Obama. His book, All We Say, is a collection of 15 speeches — from Ben Franklin to Trump — about what it means to be American.
item.content:encoded: Ben Rhodes was a speechwriter and security adviser for President Obama. His book, All We Say, is a collection of 15 speeches — from Ben Franklin to Trump — about what it means to be American.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: The movie 'Pressure' leans into the drama of high-stakes weather forecasts
item.description: The new movie tells a story about how good meteorology can literally win wars. It also takes us back in time, to when the United States was at a disadvantage when it came to weather science.
item.content:encoded:
The new movie tells a story about how good meteorology can literally win wars. It also takes us back in time, to when the United States was at a disadvantage when it came to weather science.

(Image credit: Alex Bailey)
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Dana White says it's an 'honor' to build a fight arena on the White House lawn
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dana White, president and CEO of the Ultimate Fighting Championship, about his plans to build a fighting arena on the White House lawn.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dana White, president and CEO of the Ultimate Fighting Championship, about his plans to build a fighting arena on the White House lawn.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Conservative Christians love this painting of George Washington. The event it depicts may not have happened
item.description: A painting of George Washington is being used by the Trump administration to argue the founders were devout Christians, but historians have doubts about whether the moment depicted actually happened.
item.content:encoded:
A painting of George Washington is being used by the Trump administration to argue the founders were devout Christians, but historians have doubts about whether the moment depicted actually happened.

(Image credit: First Freedom Art Company)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Why the French Open is named after Roland Garros, who didn't play tennis
item.description: Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.
item.content:encoded:
Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.

(Image credit: Branger/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Travel to Italy and Algeria in these two brilliant, translated mysteries
item.description: The End of the Sahara is a kaleidoscopic murder mystery by the Algerian writer Saïd Khatibi. An Enigma by the Sea is a witty, socially astute novel set along well-to-do Tuscan coast.
item.content:encoded: The End of the Sahara is a kaleidoscopic murder mystery by the Algerian writer Saïd Khatibi. An Enigma by the Sea is a witty, socially astute novel set along well-to-do Tuscan coast.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: If you attend a David Sedaris reading, you're helping him edit
item.description: Sedaris says the best part of reading his work to an audience is earning the laughs — or the groans. "A collective groan is fine with me," he says. His new book is The Land and Its People.
item.content:encoded:
Sedaris says the best part of reading his work to an audience is earning the laughs — or the groans. "A collective groan is fine with me," he says. His new book is The Land and Its People.

(Image credit: Anne Fishbein)
item.dc:creator: Sam Fragoso

item.title: After Stephen Colbert's viral talk show parody, CBS backs down from copyright action
item.description: CBS and Paramount backed away from copyright challenges to limit distribution of Stephen Colbert's appearance on a Michigan cable access show. He ended his run as host of "The Late Show" on Thursday.
item.content:encoded:
CBS and Paramount backed away from copyright challenges to limit distribution of Stephen Colbert's appearance on a Michigan cable access show. He ended his run as host of "The Late Show" on Thursday.

(Image credit: Evan Agostini)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: In the 'biggest book giveaway in history' WWII soldiers received pocket-sized reads
item.description: The books had to be light and small enough to fit in servicemen's pockets. The motto of the Council on Books in Wartime was: "Books Are Weapons in the War of Ideas."
item.content:encoded: The books had to be light and small enough to fit in servicemen's pockets. The motto of the Council on Books in Wartime was: "Books Are Weapons in the War of Ideas."
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan

item.title: Mind-bending photos by anonymous cousins show the pain and dreams of Afghan women
item.description: The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.
item.content:encoded:
The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.

(Image credit: Mahnaz Ebrahimi|January 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole
